notes: Sparse, source-backed quality/cost framing — NOT digitized chart points (figures are images-only on the vendor page). Prefer cost-per-successful-task over sticker $/MTok. Do not invent scores.

claims.statement: Anthropic published cost-performance curves at different effort levels (including xhigh) for Sonnet 5 vs Sonnet 4.6 vs Opus 4.8 on BrowseComp (agentic search) and OSWorld-Verified (computer use).
claims.implication: Between Sonnet 5 and Opus 4.8, adjust effort to balance cost and performance; medium effort is called out for improved cost efficiency; higher effort can match Opus 4.8 on some tasks.

claims.statement: Anthropic states Sonnet 5 is a strict improvement over Sonnet 4.6 on those curves and covers a wider range of cost-performance options than Opus 4.8.
claims.implication: Default mid-tier agent work: prefer Sonnet 5 over Sonnet 4.6; escalate effort before jumping to Opus when possible.

claims.statement: Charts price Sonnet 5 at $3/$15 per MTok (standard) and Opus 4.8 at $5/$25; intro Sonnet 5 is $2/$10 through 2026-08-31. Tokenizer change may map the same text to ~1.0–1.35× more tokens.
claims.implication: Intro pricing is roughly migration cost-neutral despite denser tokenization; after intro, re-check effective task cost.

missing: Numeric point series from the BrowseComp/OSWorld charts (not published as a table).
